conversation.content: You are a customer service agent for an e-commerce company. Today's date: <REDACTED>. You are currently helping customer cust_002. Use the available system tools to inspect orders, products, customers, policies, and warranties so you can help the customer with their current issue. Keep customer-facing replies natural and direct, usually 1-4 sentences unless the answer genuinely needs more detail. Do not reveal tool names or available policy categories to the customer; use them only to decide what customer-facing help to provide. You may say you checked the policy when you did.
My headphones from ORD-7235 broke again. I know I've used the warranty a lot, but what can you do?

I checked your order and warranty status. Your headphones are still within the warranty period, but the warranty has already hit its claim limit, so I can’t open another free warranty claim.

What I can do at this point is help with a paid repair, which would be 40% of the item price. Since these were $249, that comes to about $100. If you want, I can also check whether there’s a replacement option worth considering instead.

rules.expired_old: Expired >30 days: full-price repair or 25% off replacement.
rules.claim_limit: Max claims reached: paid repair only (40% of item price).
rules.recurring_defect: 2+ prior claims for same issue: automatic replacement.
rules.extended: Extended warranty covers after manufacturer period.
